methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $810M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
New Limit is a Series B-stage biotech and health company that has raised $170M+ to date and carries a last post-money valuation of $810M. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 3 public-market biotech and health companies (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers, with 4 same-stage private comparables directly applied.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $1.5B. Peer-Median anchors to 4 same-sector, same-stage private comparables—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—producing a median of $1.3B and a range of $600M to $3.3B. Public-Comp anchoring via P/S multiples is not applicable given the absence of disclosed revenue data for the target.

The two applicable methods converge on a valuation range of $1.1B to $1.8B, with a median of $1.4B. This implies modest appreciation from the current $810M post-money, consistent with stage progression norms. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among private peers (66% variance from median to high end) and the absence of revenue visibility, which limits cross-validation against public market multiples.
